::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Download Notebook
[Download as Jupyter Notebook](notebooks/error_budget_concept.ipynb)
:::

## Learning Objectives

After completing this tutorial, you will be able to:

- Explain why UQ results require context from other error sources
- Construct a simple error budget from discretization, parametric uncertainty, and surrogate error
- Identify when parametric UQ is the dominant uncertainty source (and when it is not)


## Prerequisites

Complete [Sensitivity Analysis](sensitivity_analysis_concept.qmd) before this tutorial.


## The Error Budget Idea

Forward UQ propagates parametric uncertainty through a model to characterize output uncertainty. But the model itself introduces errors that have nothing to do with parameter uncertainty:

1. **Discretization error** --- the numerical mesh or time step introduces approximation error relative to the exact PDE solution
2. **Parametric uncertainty** --- the spread in the QoI due to uncertain inputs (this is what UQ measures)
3. **Surrogate approximation error** --- if we use a PCE or GP instead of the true model, the surrogate introduces additional error

The **error budget** compares these three sources. If discretization error dominates parametric uncertainty, then refining the parameter distributions or running more UQ samples will not improve the prediction --- the mesh must be refined first. Conversely, if parametric uncertainty dominates, the UQ results are meaningful and mesh refinement has diminishing returns.



## Visualizing the Error Budget

@fig-error-budget-bar compares the three error sources for the beam tip deflection model. The bars show the magnitude of each error source relative to the QoI value.

::: {#cell-fig-error-budget-bar .cell execution_count=2}

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Error budget for beam tip deflection. Discretization error is the difference between coarse (5 elements) and fine mesh solutions. Parametric uncertainty is the 2-sigma spread from uncertain KLE inputs. Surrogate error is the RMSE of a degree-2 PCE.](error_budget_concept_files/figure-html/fig-error-budget-bar-output-1.png){#fig-error-budget-bar width=759 height=466}
:::
:::


## When Is UQ Meaningful?

@fig-budget-scenarios illustrates two contrasting scenarios by varying the mesh resolution. When the mesh is coarse, discretization error dominates and the UQ spread is misleading. When the mesh is fine, parametric uncertainty dominates and the UQ results are trustworthy.

::: {#cell-fig-budget-scenarios .cell execution_count=3}

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Two scenarios. Left: coarse mesh (5 elements) --- discretization error dominates, making parametric UQ misleading. Right: fine mesh (40 elements) --- parametric uncertainty dominates, so the UQ spread is meaningful.](error_budget_concept_files/figure-html/fig-budget-scenarios-output-1.png){#fig-budget-scenarios width=1143 height=421}
:::
:::


::: {.callout-important}
## Practical Rules

Before trusting UQ results, check the error budget:

1. **Refine the mesh** until discretization error is much smaller than parametric uncertainty ($\epsilon_\text{disc} \ll 2\sigma_\text{param}$)
2. **Increase surrogate degree** until surrogate error is much smaller than parametric uncertainty ($\epsilon_\text{surr} \ll 2\sigma_\text{param}$)
3. **Only then** are the UQ statistics (mean, variance, Sobol indices) meaningful

If discretization error dominates, the apparent parametric uncertainty is an artifact of an inaccurate model --- not a genuine characterization of the QoI spread.
:::


## Key Takeaways

- UQ results are only meaningful when **parametric uncertainty dominates** other error sources
- An **error budget** compares discretization error, parametric uncertainty, and surrogate approximation error
- Refine the mesh and surrogate until their errors are small compared to the parametric spread
- Always check the error budget before presenting UQ results to decision makers


## Exercises

1. **(Easy)** From @fig-error-budget-bar, which error source dominates? What would you do first to improve the analysis?

2. **(Medium)** Increase the PCE degree from 2 to 4 and recompute the surrogate error. By how much does it decrease? Is the error budget now dominated by a single source?

3. **(Challenge)** Create an error budget for a different QoI (e.g., integrated bending stress instead of tip deflection). Does the relative importance of the three error sources change?


## Next Steps

- [Sobol Sensitivity Analysis](sobol_sensitivity_analysis.qmd) --- Monte Carlo estimation of Sobol indices
